author: @blumstein3468
text:
Still in the middle of digesting the video's breakdown of Pontus' kit. I learned a lot more than I expected, in particular with the scaling and additive/multiplicative interactions of his Strength Down from Enlightens, Wheels, etc.

I was also struck by the statement that Morimens is a 'maths' game; I'm so used to gachas being "level up, kit out, watch big numbers" games that actively diving deep into the Awakeners' kit and even the enemies' stats are difficult for me to adopt. Not impossible, but some part of me still resists to do so and I waste half an hour or so of effort because I brought Vortice into a cat fight that seals other Awakeners' Exalt after the first one uses theirs. It's growing pains, but I hope I do learn - correctly - from these experiences, not just in-game but also outside of it.

As an Awakener pre-release review video, this has both my thumbs-up. Exhaustive analysis, concise explanations, and very appreciated blurbs about in-game trends on mechanics and Awakene
video_title: A Scarily Lengthy Analysis of Pontos in Morimens

author: @SleepyGuppy
text:
He's one of the most interesting characters in terms of how his kit age, given that he's the first limited unit in the game, with him being regarded as of the most debated on how relevant he his in end game (besides his OE).

With the launch of GMurphy and Lemurian buffs, he's gone up as one of the core units of the team, if only 2rd best of the old Lemurians to Miryam.

One thing of how OE works is that it makes Tentacles actually attack 3 times, not 2, as the effect should be "Command all Tentacles to attack once at turn end." for it to count as 2 times of turn end Tentacles attacks, not 3.
video_title: Tulu: The Bored King #morimens

author: @blumstein3468
text:
I didn't use Tulu for my first 350 a few days ago--my account was 36 days old at that point. Aside from a lack of resources I couldn't make sense of his kit. I just tried him out yesterday against the Catto and he surprised me with how strong he is!

It kind of scares me how THIS guy is now considered a sub-dps to GMurphy, 'cause it's ridiculous to have your Assault play second fiddle to the Chorus unit in your team. But I can see how it happened with the damage difference and how his cards enhance the tentacles. I keep resetting from my inexperience with the whole Divine Aequor/Sacrifice thing going on, though, so I'll just leave Tulu for my Arc 1 Hard Corpita team for now...

Thanks for the Tulu guide! Your explanation of the mechanics are very digestible. I look forward to your version of explaining Tawil and 24, as their kits have more nuance than their skill descriptions show.
video_title: Tulu: The Bored King #morimens

text:
completely ignoring how much of an attack on me and my type Pontos is, ever since I saw the str down i KNEW i needed him [for context. even before i saw he he and his kit were announced. i've been flopping so bad at trying to beat arc2 5-15 and I've been trying to figure out a way to put on a str down character into my team so i can survive attacks that are otherwise actively bigger than my max health EVEN WITH A MAX HEALTH BASED TEAM BC I MAIN SALVADOR.] oh that Nefraea... i actually do love her as a character but oh my god she's making my life so difficult rn /silly

thank you so much for this analysis !! i fear these days all my ability to critically think have been used up in other applications rather than my fun and whimsical games and i eat this up as I wait with anticipation for Pontos drop!!

author: @karaboutsleepy
text: A little niche thing I found with Tulu's 4 cost card is that when used on an enemy with Barrier it deletes all barrier stacks while still doing damage. And no, I don't mean the amount of tentacles you get can delete all of the stacks of barrier. No. I mean the single green eye beam attack can delete all stacks of barrier while still doing piercing damage and then the tentacles come right after. Pretty niche and pretty useful!

text: Hace unos años jugué y termine la VN de Saya no uta en el celular con un emulador y fue toda una experiencia, juego muchos gachas y conocía de nombre Morimens pero cuando ví la colab que iba a salir quedé :O porque hace bastante que ninguna colab me generaba expectativa o hype (siempre era nier, fate, re zero, cosas así) solo reverse x assasins creed hace un año me gustó mucho. En fin probé el morimens por una semana pero no me quedé, fue bueno ver a Saya y escuchar otra canción de Kanako Itō, mas colaboraciones así por favor.
